    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | External ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | — |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `StripePaymentService`, `StripeRefundService`, `StripeWebhookHandler` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Stripe, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (admin/ reporting only — NOT used in FR5/FR6 payment flows; JS SDK widget only creates orders)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews (engagement only — FR8 attendance verification)\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — INACTIVE for payments — all refunds go through `StripeRefundService`; original EB refund read-only API has no submission endpoint\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Stripe ACL Facades (payments-ticketing only)\n\nAll 3 facades are in `shared/stripe/service/`. Only `payments-ticketing` calls these:\n\n1. **StripePaymentService** — Create PaymentIntents; confirm and capture payments\n2. **StripeRefundService** — Submit refunds; query refund status\n3. **StripeWebhookHandler** — Verify webhook signatures; parse `payment_intent.succeeded` / `payment_intent.payment_failed` / `charge.refunded` events\n\n**Hard Rule:** Never call `com.stripe.*` SDK classes directly from a module service — always delegate to the facade in `shared/stripe/service/`.\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. ## Entry 448
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T20:23:16.278Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T20:23:16.278Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_GnoiqxRSZn1kQCHjWDqeccOC",
    "output": "Chunk ID: f7e5dd\nWall time: 0.0510 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1285\nOutput:\n# TEST_AGENT.md — Testing Standards & Execution Agent\n\n> Read this file before writing or running tests.\n> This agent is responsible for test code implementation, execution, failure fixing, and result logging.\n\n---\n\n## Purpose\n\nThis file defines the responsibilities of the **Test Agent**.\n\nThe **SPEC_GENERATOR** is the sole source of truth for what to test — it writes all test cases in Stage 8 of the `SPEC_*.md` file. The **TEST_AGENT does not decide what to test**. Its only job is to:\n\n- **Translate** the test cases written in `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8 into executable test code\n- **Run** those tests\n- **Fix** failures caused by new or changed code\n- **Log** results in a timestamped report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Scope Ownership\n\n### Test Agent MUST\n\n- Read Stage 8 of the `SPEC_*.md` file and implement exactly those test cases — nothing more, nothing less\n- Write test code for all layers as specified in the test cases:\n  - `domain/` (pure unit)\n  - `service/` (Mockito orchestration)\n  - `api/` (`@WebMvcTest` HTTP contract)\n  - `arch/` (ArchUnit boundaries)\n- Run relevant tests after implementation\n- Fix failing tests and related test setup issues\n- Fix production code only when required to satisfy approved spec behavior\n- Log all found errors and all applied fixes in a test report file\n\n### Test Agent MUST NOT\n\n- Decide what test cases to write — that is SPEC_GENERATOR's job\n- Add, remove, or modify test cases from Stage 8\n- Introduce unapproved features not present in `SPEC_*.md`\n- Change module ownership or dependency direction rules\n- Add module-level `@ControllerAdvice`\n- Skip failed tests without documenting reason and impact\n\n---\n\n## Inputs (Required Before Writing Tests)\n\n1. Confirmed feature spec with Stage 8 test cases: `SPEC_*.md` ← **primary input**\n2. Module ownership and boundaries: `PRODUCT.md`\n3. Layer/package standards: `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md`\n4. Test patterns/rules: `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md`\n5. Coding constraints for compatibility: `agents/CODING_AGENT.md`\n\n---\n\n## Test Design Standards\n\n### AAA Format (Mandatory)\n\nAll tests must follow Arrange–Act–Assert.\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_400_when_cityId_missing() {\n    // Arrange\n    MockHttpServletRequestBuilder request = get(\"/api/v1/catalog/venues\");\n\n    // Act\n    ResultActions result = mockMvc.perform(request);\n\n    // Assert\n    result.andExpect(status().isBadRequest());\n}\n```\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`should_[expected_behaviour]_when_[condition]`\n\nExamples:\n- `should_soft_delete_event_when_missing_from_org_sync`\n- `should_return_401_when_webhook_signature_invalid`\n- `should_not_query_db_when_cache_hit`\n\n### Layer Rules\n\n- `domain/`: no Spring context, no mocks for infra\n- `service/`: Mockito only (`@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)`)\n- `api/`: `@WebMvcTest`, services/mappers mocked\n- `arch/`: ArchUnit boundary checks based on hard rules\n\n---\n\n## Execution Workflow\n\n1. Read Stage 8 test cases from the approved `SPEC_*.md` — do not interpret or expand them\n2. Implement each test case as code in the correct layer\n3. Run focused tests first (changed files only), then full module suite\n4. Capture failures\n5. Fix test or code issues\n6. Re-run until green (or blocked)\n7. Produce and save test report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Failure Triage Rules\n\nWhen tests fail, classify and resolve in this order:\n\n1. **Test setup issue** (mock, fixture, context) → fix test\n2. **Contract mismatch** (spec vs endpoint/DTO) → fix code or test to match approved spec\n3. **Behavior bug** (business logic wrong) → fix production code\n4. **Architecture violation** (ArchUnit) → fix package/dependency layering\n5. **External/infra flake** → document as blocked with reproducible evidence\n\nDo not silently ignore failing tests.\n\n---\n\n## Required Result Logging\n\nAfter every test cycle, generate a result log file in:\n\n`test_result_folder/`\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`[feature_or_flow]_[yyyyMMdd_HHmmss].md`\n\nExamples:\n- `fr1_catalog_search_20260303_201530.md`\n- `payment_confirmation_flow_20260303_204012.md`\n\n### Log Template (Mandatory)\n\n```markdown\n# Test Result — [Feature/Flow]\n\n- Timestamp: [yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss]\n- Spec: [SPEC_*.md]\n- Module(s): [module list]\n- Commit/Working State: [hash or local]\n\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\n- Total planned:\n- Implemented:\n- Passed:\n- Failed:\n- Skipped:\n\n## Failures Found\n1. [test name]\n   - Error:\n   - Root cause:\n   - Fix applied:\n\n## Code/Test Changes Applied\n- [file path] — [short change summary]\n\n## Final Status\n- [PASS | PARTIAL | BLOCKED]\n- Notes:\n```\n\n---\n\n## Completion Criteria\n\nTest Agent is complete only when:\n\n- All Stage 8 test cases from `SPEC_*.md` are implemented as code\n- All implemented tests are executed\n- Failures are fixed or explicitly documented as blocked\n- A timestamped result log is saved in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Handoff Contract\n\n- `SPEC_GENERATOR` defines what to test — writes all test cases in Stage 8\n- `CODING_AGENT` implements production code\n- `TEST_AGENT` writes test code for Stage 8 cases, runs them, fixes failures, and publishes the final test report"
  }
}
```

Read Stage 8 test cases from the approved `SPEC_*.md` — do not interpret or expand them\n2. Implement each test case as code in the correct layer\n3. Run focused tests first (changed files only), then full module suite\n4. Capture failures\n5. Fix test or code issues\n6. Re-run until green (or blocked)\n7. Produce and save test report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Failure Triage Rules\n\nWhen tests fail, classify and resolve in this order:\n\n1. **Test setup issue** (mock, fixture, context) → fix test\n2. **Contract mismatch** (spec vs endpoint/DTO) → fix code or test to match approved spec\n3. **Behavior bug** (business logic wrong) → fix production code\n4. **Architecture violation** (ArchUnit) → fix package/dependency layering\n5. **External/infra flake** → document as blocked with reproducible evidence\n\nDo not silently ignore failing tests.\n\n---\n\n## Required Result Logging\n\nAfter every test cycle, generate a result log file in:\n\n`test_result_folder/`\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`[feature_or_flow]_[yyyyMMdd_HHmmss].md`\n\nExamples:\n- `fr1_catalog_search_20260303_201530.md`\n- `payment_confirmation_flow_20260303_204012.md`\n\n### Log Template (Mandatory)\n\n```markdown\n# Test Result — [Feature/Flow]\n\n- Timestamp: [yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss]\n- Spec: [SPEC_*.md]\n- Module(s): [module list]\n- Commit/Working State: [hash or local]\n\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\n- Total planned:\n- Implemented:\n- Passed:\n- Failed:\n- Skipped:\n\n## Failures Found\n1. ## Entry 449
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T20:23:16.364Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T20:23:16.364Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_UvV1W2hdIATCUTo6UBYw5Mp3",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 044e5a\nWall time: 0.0520 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2788\nOutput:\n# Testing Guide — Modular Monolith\n\n> Tests are not optional. Every feature must have coverage across all four layers.\n> The `arch/` tests in particular are self-enforcing — they fail the build automatically\n> if anyone violates a module boundary. Never delete or weaken an arch test.\n\n---\n\n## Core Principle — Each Layer Tests One Thing\n\nThe most common testing mistake is putting the wrong test in the wrong layer.\nUse this rule: **each layer tests exactly one concern.**\n\n| Layer | Tests One Concern | Spring Context? | Key Annotation |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `domain/` | Business rules on the domain object | None | Plain JUnit 5 |\n| `service/` | Orchestration — right calls in right order | None | `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)` |\n| `api/` | HTTP contract — status codes, JSON shape, validation | Web layer only | `@WebMvcTest` |\n| `arch/` | Architectural boundaries | None | `@AnalyzeClasses` |\n| `mapper/` | Field translation correctness | None | Plain JUnit 5 |\n\n---\n\n## Test Folder Structure\n\nEvery module must mirror its source structure exactly:\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/\n    ├── main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    │   ├── api/controller/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   ├── service/\n    │   ├── repository/\n    │   ├── mapper/\n    │   └── event/\n    └── test/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n        ├── domain/        ← Pure unit tests. No Spring. No infrastructure mocks.\n        ├── service/       ← Mockito only. Mocks repositories and ACL facades.\n        ├── api/           ← @WebMvcTest slice. Mocks service and mapper.\n        ├── arch/          ← ArchUnit. Enforces module boundary rules at build time.\n        └── mapper/        ← MapStruct translation tests. No Spring.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Test Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\nAll test methods must follow this exact pattern:\n\n```\nshould_[expected_behaviour]_when_[condition]\n```\n\nThe name must be readable as a sentence describing the behaviour being verified.\nNever use vague names like `testConfirm()`, `test1()`, or `happyPath()`.\n\n**Good examples:**\n```\nshould_confirm_booking_when_status_is_pending\nshould_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_confirming_a_cancelled_booking\nshould_return_200_when_booking_is_found\nshould_return_400_when_seat_count_exceeds_maximum\nshould_return_404_when_booking_does_not_exist\nshould_publish_BookingConfirmedEvent_after_successful_confirmation\nshould_throw_BookingNotFoundException_when_booking_id_is_unknown\nshould_map_confirmed_booking_to_response_with_correct_status\n```\n\n**Bad examples (never use):**\n```\ntestConfirm()\ntest_happy_path()\nconfirmWorks()\nbooking_test_1()\n```\n\n---\n\n## Layer 1 — `domain/` Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nVerify that the domain object enforces its own rules correctly.\nThese are the fastest tests in the suite — they must run in milliseconds.\n\n### What to Test Here\n\n- Valid state transitions succeed and produce the correct new state\n- Invalid state transitions throw `BusinessRuleException` with the correct message\n- Guard conditions (preconditions on methods) are enforced\n- Value object construction validates its invariants\n- Terminal states cannot be transitioned out of\n- Null or invalid inputs are rejected\n\n### What NOT to Test Here\n\n- Persistence (no repositories, no DB)\n- HTTP (no controllers, no MockMvc)\n- Spring beans (no `@SpringBootTest`, no `@ExtendWith(SpringExtension.class)`)\n- Mocks of infrastructure — if you need a mock here, the domain has an infrastructure\n  dependency. Fix the domain, not the test.\n\n### Annotations\n\nNone. Plain JUnit 5 only.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct — no annotations on the class\nclass BookingTest {\n    ...\n}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — domain tests never load Spring\n@SpringBootTest\nclass BookingTest { ... }\n\n// ❌ Wrong — if you need this, your domain imports infrastructure\n@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)\nclass BookingTest { ... }\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path Tests\n\nTest that the domain method succeeds and produces the correct outcome under valid conditions.\nCover every valid state transition defined in the spec's state machine.\n\n```java\nclass BookingTest {\n\n    // Happy path: valid transition succeeds\n    @Test\n    void should_confirm_booking_when_status_is_pending() {\n        // Arrange — set up the entity in a valid starting state\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.PENDING);\n\n        // Act — call the domain method\n        booking.confirm();\n\n        // Assert — verify the expected outcome\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_cancel_booking_when_status_is_pending() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.PENDING);\n\n        booking.cancel();\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_cancel_booking_when_status_is_confirmed_and_event_is_more_than_48h_away() {\n        LocalDateTime eventTime = LocalDateTime.now().plusDays(3);\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED, eventTime);\n\n        booking.cancel();\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests\n\nTest that invalid operations are rejected with the correct exception and message.\nCover every forbidden transition in the spec's state machine.\n\n```java\nclass BookingTest {\n\n    // Negative path: invalid transition throws BusinessRuleException\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_confirming_a_cancelled_booking() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::confirm)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Cannot confirm a cancelled booking\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_confirming_an_already_confirmed_booking() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::confirm)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Booking is already confirmed\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_cancelling_within_48h_of_event() {\n        LocalDateTime eventTime = LocalDateTime.now().plusHours(24); // inside the 48h window\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED, eventTime);\n\n should_throw_when_operating_on_a_terminal_cancelled_state() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n\n        // All operations on a terminal state must be rejected\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::confirm).isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class);\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::cancel).isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class);\n Cancellation Refunds\n\n**Owner:** payments-ticketing  \n**Module(s):** `payments-ticketing` (primary), `admin/` (orchestration stub), `scheduling` (slot cancel source), `booking-inventory` (seat release consumer), `shared/stripe/` (refund ACL)  \n**Flow:** Flow 6 — Cancellation Policy Definition → Individual Item Cancel → Full Booking Cancel (Policy-driven) → Organizer Event Cancel → Batch Refund Stub  \n**Last Updated:** March 5, 2026  \n**Status:** Stages 1–8 Complete — Ready for implementation planning\n\n**Depends on:**\n- `SPEC_5.md` (FR5) — booking/payment/refund/ticket baseline\n- `SPEC_4.md` (FR4) — cart assembly and seat lock lifecycle\n- `SPEC_3.MD` (FR3) — JWT auth/ownership enforcement\n- `SPEC_2.md` (FR2) — scheduling owns slot lifecycle and publishes slot cancellation signals\n\n**Scope Clarification (Requested):**\n- Includes both cancellation cases:\n  1) **Individual ticket/item cancellation** within a booking\n  2) **Entire event cancellation** (organizer/admin initiated)\n- **Wallet credit is explicitly out of scope** for this flow.\n- Cancellation policy must include **window**, **refund percentage**, and **refund value computation**.\n- Event-cancel refund processing is **stubbed** for now (integration-safe placeholder design).\n\n---\n\n## Stage 1 — Requirements\n\n### Area A — Cancellation Policy Management\n\n- `[MUST]` System must support a DB-backed `CancellationPolicy` per organization.\n- `[MUST]` Policy must be tier-based with cancellation windows and percentages.\n- `[MUST]` Each tier defines: `hoursBeforeEvent` + `refundPercent`.\n- `[MUST]` Refund amount for cancellation must be derived from policy tiers and booking/item amount.\n- `[MUST]` A system default policy must exist as fallback when org policy is absent.\n- `[MUST]` Policy create/update endpoints must be admin-only.\n- `[SHOULD]` Policy retrieval endpoint should always return effective policy (org or fallback default).\n- `[WONT]` Per-seat, per-ticket-class, or wallet-credit-specific policy rules in FR6.\n\n### Area B — Individual Ticket/Item Cancellation (Buyer-Initiated)\n\n- `[MUST]` Buyer can cancel one or more `BookingItem`s from a `CONFIRMED` booking.\n- `[MUST]` Item cancel must support partial cancellation (remaining items continue as active).\n- `[MUST]` Cancelled item(s) must transition `BookingItem.status: ACTIVE → CANCELLED`.\n- `[MUST]` Related e-ticket(s) must transition `ETicket.status: ACTIVE → VOIDED`.\n- `[MUST]` Refund must be policy-driven using applicable cancellation window and percentage.\n- `[MUST]` If all items are cancelled via item-cancel flow, booking must end in `CANCELLED`.\n- `[MUST]` Duplicate cancellation for same active item must be prevented.\n- `[MUST]` `BookingCancelledEvent` must publish only cancelled `seatIds` for seat release.\n- `[WONT]` Wallet credit payout route for buyer cancellation in FR6.\n\n### Area C — Full Booking Cancellation (Buyer-Initiated)\n\n- `[MUST]` Existing booking cancel path remains available.\n- `[MUST]` Full cancel must use `CancellationPolicy` (not static config fee-percent).\n- `[MUST]` If applicable refund percentage is 0, booking still cancels and tickets are voided.\n- `[MUST]` Zero-refund case must persist a refund record with `amount=0` and terminal success semantics.\n\n### Area D — Event-Level Cancellation (Organizer/Admin-Initiated)\n\n- `[MUST]` Admin/organizer event cancellation starts in scheduling/admin flow and emits slot/event cancellation signal.\n- `[MUST]` `payments-ticketing` must consume cancellation signal and process all affected confirmed bookings.\n- `[MUST]` Event-level cancellation refunds must be **100%**, regardless of buyer policy windows.\n- `[MUST]` Per-booking event-cancel handling must cancel booking, cancel items, void tickets, and release seats.\n- `[MUST]` Event cancellation batch processing must tolerate per-booking failures (continue processing others).\n- `[MUST]` Event-cancel Stripe refund execution is stubbed in FR6 with explicit TODO seam.\n- `[WONT]` Organizer notification campaign workflows and wallet credits in FR6.\n\n### Area E — Integration & Boundary Rules\n\n- `[MUST]` Modules cannot call Stripe SDK directly outside shared ACL (`StripeRefundService`).\n- `[MUST]` No cross-module entity/service imports.\n- `[MUST]` Events contain only primitives/IDs/value fields, no entities.\n- `[MUST]` Admin module stays orchestration-only (no domain tables).\n\n---\n\n## Stage 2 — Domain Model\n\n### Core Aggregate Behavior\n\nA `Booking` can be partially cancelled at item granularity while remaining `CONFIRMED` if at least one item stays active. If all items become cancelled, booking reaches `CANCELLED`. Refunds are policy-driven for buyer-initiated cancels and always full for event-level cancel.\n\n### Entities\n\n**Existing (extended):**\n\n- **`Booking`**\n  - Add `orgId` (for policy resolution)\n  - Add `slotStartTime` (cached at booking creation)\n  - Lifecycle remains: `PENDING → CONFIRMED → CANCELLATION_PENDING → CANCELLED`\n\n- **`BookingItem`**\n  - Lifecycle: `ACTIVE → CANCELLED`\n  - Cancellation now independently addressable by item ID\n\n- **`ETicket`**\n  - Lifecycle: `ACTIVE → VOIDED`\n\n- **`Refund`**\n  - Add cancellation source type: `BUYER_PARTIAL`, `BUYER_FULL`, `EVENT_CANCELLED`\n\n- **`CancellationRequest`**\n  - Extended to support item-level tracking (`bookingItemId` nullable for backward compatibility)\n  - Lifecycle: `PENDING → APPROVED | REJECTED`\n\n**New:**\n\n- **`CancellationPolicy`**\n  - Fields: `id`, `orgId` (nullable for system default), `scope`, timestamps\n\n- **`CancellationPolicyTier`**\n  - Fields: `policyId`, `hoursBeforeEvent`, `refundPercent`, `sortOrder`\n\n- **`EventCancellationRefundAudit`** (stub-focused operational record)\n  - Tracks each affected booking during event-level cancel batch run\n  - Fields: `slotId`, `bookingId`, `status`, `errorMessage`, `processedAt`\n\n### Non-Persisted Value Objects\n\n- `RefundCalculationResult(refundPercent, refundAmountInSmallestUnit, tierLabel)`\n- `EventCancellationBatchContext(slotId, orgId, affectedBookingCount)`\n\n### Cross-Module References (IDs only)\n\n| Field | Points To | Module |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Booking.userId` | users.id | identity |\n| `Booking.cartId` | carts.id | booking-inventory |\n| `Booking.slotId` | show_slots.id | scheduling |\n| `Booking.orgId` | organization id | identity/scheduling context |\n| `BookingItem.seatId` | seats.id | booking-inventory |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 3 — Architecture & File Structure\n\n### Module Ownership\n\n- **Primary ownership:** `payments-ticketing`\n- **Admin trigger (stub endpoint):** `admin/`\n- **Slot cancellation source of truth:** `scheduling`\n- **Seat release consumer:** `booking-inventory`\n\n### Interface vs HTTP Calls Plan (Hotspot Prevention)\n\n#### 1) Use Interface (in-process abstraction) where read is frequent and local-contract stable\n\n- Use shared reader interface for payment confirmation checks already established in FR5 (`PaymentConfirmationReader`).\n- Use domain services/repositories in `payments-ticketing` for cancellation execution and policy resolution.\n\n#### 2) Use HTTP only for cross-module command/read boundaries that are not hot-loop paths\n\n- `admin/` → `scheduling` cancel command via REST (single command per admin action).\n- No per-item or per-booking hot-loop HTTP calls inside cancellation batch.\n\n#### 3) Hotspot assessment\n\n- **Expected hotspot risk in FR6 is low** because cancellation paths primarily operate on `payments-ticketing` local DB state.\n- Potential throughput concentration point is event-level batch refund fan-out to Stripe.\n- Mitigation in FR6 design:\n  - Process booking refunds independently (failure isolation)\n  - Add audit table for retries/visibility\n  - Keep Stripe integration behind one service seam (`StripeRefundService`)\n- Conclusion: **no architectural hotspot in module communication**; only external refund throughput sensitivity (expected and controllable).\n\n### New/Changed Files (planned)\n\n```text\npayments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/\n\ndomain/\n├── CancellationPolicy.java\n├── CancellationPolicyTier.java\n├── EventCancellationRefundAudit.java\n└── enums/\n    ├── CancellationPolicyScope.java\n    └── CancellationPolicyMapper.java\n\nexception/\n├── CancellationPolicyNotFoundException.java\n└── DuplicateItemCancellationException.java\n```\n\n```text\nadmin/src/main/java/.../api/controller/\n└── AdminSlotCancelController.java (stub orchestration endpoint)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Stage 4 — DB Schema\n\n### New Tables\n\n#### `cancellation_policies`\n\n```sql\ncancellation_policies (\n  id                  BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n  org_id              BIGINT,\n  scope               VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,   -- ORG | SYSTEM_DEFAULT\n  created_by_admin_id BIGINT,\n  created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\n  updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()\n)\nINDEXES: idx_cancellation_policies_org_id\nCONSTRAINTS:\n  - uq_cancellation_policies_org_id UNIQUE (org_id) WHERE org_id IS NOT NULL\n  - uq_cancellation_policies_default UNIQUE (scope) WHERE scope = 'SYSTEM_DEFAULT'\n```\n\n#### `cancellation_policy_tiers`\n\n```sql\ncancellation_policy_tiers (\n  id                 BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n  policy_id          BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES cancellation_policies(id),\n  hours_before_event INTEGER,\n  refund_percent     INTEGER NOT NULL,\n  sort_order         INTEGER NOT NULL,\n  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()\n)\nINDEXES: idx_policy_tiers_policy_id, idx_policy_tiers_sort_order\nCONSTRAINTS:\n  - chk_refund_percent_range CHECK (refund_percent >= 0 AND refund_percent <= 100)\n  - uq_policy_tier_sort UNIQUE (policy_id, sort_order)\n```\n\n#### `event_cancellation_refund_audits`\n\n```sql\nevent_cancellation_refund_audits (\n  id             BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n  slot_id        BIGINT NOT NULL,\n  booking_id     BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES bookings(id),\n  refund_id      BIGINT REFERENCES refunds(id),\n  status         VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL, -- PENDING | SUCCEEDED | FAILED\n  error_message  TEXT,\n  processed_at   TIMESTAMPTZ,\n  created_at     TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()\n)\nINDEXES: idx_event_cancel_audit_slot_id, idx_event_cancel_audit_booking_id\nCONSTRAINTS:\n  - uq_event_cancel_slot_booking UNIQUE (slot_id, booking_id)\n```\n\n### Alter Existing Tables\n\n#### `bookings`\n\n```sql\nALTER TABLE bookings\n  ADD COLUMN org_id BIGINT,\n  ADD COLUMN slot_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ;\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_bookings_org_id ON bookings(org_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_bookings_slot_start_time ON bookings(slot_start_time);\n```\n\n#### `refunds`\n\n```sql\nALTER TABLE refunds\n  ADD COLUMN cancellation_type VARCHAR(30);\n```\n\n#### `cancellation_requests`\n\n```sql\nALTER TABLE cancellation_requests\n  ADD COLUMN booking_item_id BIGINT REFERENCES booking_items(id);\n\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_cancellation_requests_item_pending\n  ON cancellation_requests(booking_item_id)\n  WHERE booking_item_id IS NOT NULL AND status = 'PENDING';\n```\n\n### Migrations\n\n- `V33__create_cancellation_policy_tables.sql`\n- `V34__extend_booking_refund_cancellation_request_for_fr6.sql`\n- `V35__create_event_cancellation_refund_audit.sql`\n\n### Seed Data (V33)\n\nSystem default tiers (example baseline):\n- `>=72h`: `100%`\n- `>=24h and <72h`: `50%`\n- `<24h`: `0%`\n\n---\n\n"

}

Refund Stub  \n**Last Updated:** March 5, 2026  \n**Status:** Stages 1–8 Complete — Ready for implementation planning\n\n**Depends on:**\n- `SPEC_5.md` (FR5) — booking/payment/refund/ticket baseline\n- `SPEC_4.md` (FR4) — cart assembly and seat lock lifecycle\n- `SPEC_3.MD` (FR3) — JWT auth/ownership enforcement\n- `SPEC_2.md` (FR2) — scheduling owns slot lifecycle and publishes slot cancellation signals\n\n**Scope Clarification (Requested):**\n- Includes both cancellation cases:\n  1) **Individual ticket/item cancellation** within a booking\n  2) **Entire event cancellation** (organizer/admin initiated)\n- **Wallet credit is explicitly out of scope** for this flow.\n- Cancellation policy must include **window**, **refund percentage**, and **refund value computation**.\n- Event-cancel refund processing is **stubbed** for now (integration-safe placeholder design).\n\n---\n\n## Stage 1 — Requirements\n\n### Area A — Cancellation Policy Management\n\n- `[MUST]` System must support a DB-backed `CancellationPolicy` per organization.\n- `[MUST]` Policy must be tier-based with cancellation windows and percentages.\n- `[MUST]` Each tier defines: `hoursBeforeEvent` + `refundPercent`.\n- `[MUST]` Refund amount for cancellation must be derived from policy tiers and booking/item amount.\n- `[MUST]` A system default policy must exist as fallback when org policy is absent.\n- `[MUST]` Policy create/update endpoints must be admin-only.\n- `[SHOULD]` Policy retrieval endpoint should always return effective policy (org or fallback default).\n- `[WONT]` Per-seat, per-ticket-class, or wallet-credit-specific policy rules in FR6.\n\n### Area B — Individual Ticket/Item Cancellation (Buyer-Initiated)\n\n- `[MUST]` Buyer can cancel one or more `BookingItem`s from a `CONFIRMED` booking.\n- `[MUST]` Item cancel must support partial cancellation (remaining items continue as active).\n- `[MUST]` Cancelled item(s) must transition `BookingItem.status: ACTIVE → CANCELLED`.\n- `[MUST]` Related e-ticket(s) must transition `ETicket.status: ACTIVE → VOIDED`.\n- `[MUST]` Refund must be policy-driven using applicable cancellation window and percentage.\n- `[MUST]` If all items are cancelled via item-cancel flow, booking must end in `CANCELLED`.\n- `[MUST]` Duplicate cancellation for same active item must be prevented.\n- `[MUST]` `BookingCancelledEvent` must publish only cancelled `seatIds` for seat release.\n- `[WONT]` Wallet credit payout route for buyer cancellation in FR6.\n\n### Area C — Full Booking Cancellation (Buyer-Initiated)\n\n- `[MUST]` Existing booking cancel path remains available.\n- `[MUST]` Full cancel must use `CancellationPolicy` (not static config fee-percent).\n- `[MUST]` If applicable refund percentage is 0, booking still cancels and tickets are voided.\n- `[MUST]` Zero-refund case must persist a refund record with `amount=0` and terminal success semantics.\n\n### Area D — Event-Level Cancellation (Organizer/Admin-Initiated)\n\n- `[MUST]` Admin/organizer event cancellation starts in scheduling/admin flow and emits slot/event cancellation signal.\n- `[MUST]` `payments-ticketing` must consume cancellation signal and process all affected confirmed bookings.\n- `[MUST]` Event-level cancellation refunds must be **100%**, regardless of buyer policy windows.\n- `[MUST]` Per-booking event-cancel handling must cancel booking, cancel items, void tickets, and release seats.\n- `[MUST]` Event cancellation batch processing must tolerate per-booking failures (continue processing others).\n- `[MUST]` Event-cancel Stripe refund execution is stubbed in FR6 with explicit TODO seam.\n- `[WONT]` Organizer notification campaign workflows and wallet credits in FR6.\n\n### Area E — Integration & Boundary Rules\n\n- `[MUST]` Modules cannot call Stripe SDK directly outside shared ACL (`StripeRefundService`).\n- `[MUST]` No cross-module entity/service imports.\n- `[MUST]` Events contain only primitives/IDs/value fields, no entities.\n- `[MUST]` Admin module stays orchestration-only (no domain tables).\n\n---\n\n## Stage 2 — Domain Model\n\n### Core Aggregate Behavior\n\nA `Booking` can be partially cancelled at item granularity while remaining `CONFIRMED` if at least one item stays active. If all items become cancelled, booking reaches `CANCELLED`. Refunds are policy-driven for buyer-initiated cancels and always full for event-level cancel.\n\n### Entities\n\n**Existing (extended):**\n\n- **`Booking`**\n  - Add `orgId` (for policy resolution)\n  - Add `slotStartTime` (cached at booking creation)\n  - Lifecycle remains: `PENDING → CONFIRMED → CANCELLATION_PENDING → CANCELLED`\n\n- **`BookingItem`**\n  - Lifecycle: `ACTIVE → CANCELLED`\n  - Cancellation now independently addressable by item ID\n\n- **`ETicket`**\n  - Lifecycle: `ACTIVE → VOIDED`\n\n- **`Refund`**\n  - Add cancellation source type: `BUYER_PARTIAL`, `BUYER_FULL`, `EVENT_CANCELLED`\n\n- **`CancellationRequest`**\n  - Extended to support item-level tracking (`bookingItemId` nullable for backward compatibility)\n  - Lifecycle: `PENDING → APPROVED | REJECTED`\n\n**New:**\n\n- **`CancellationPolicy`**\n  - Fields: `id`, `orgId` (nullable for system default), `scope`, timestamps\n\n- **`CancellationPolicyTier`**\n  - Fields: `policyId`, should_throw_when_cancelling_booking_already_cancelled() {\n        CancellationService service = createService(\"http://localhost:9\", 24, 0);\n        Booking booking = confirmedBooking(91L, \"BK-20260304-001\", 31L, 11L, 21L, 150000L);\n        booking.markCancellationPending();\n        booking.cancel();\n        when(bookingRepository.findByBookingRef(\"BK-20260304-001\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.cancel(31L, \"BK-20260304-001\", when(bookingRepository.findByBookingRef(\"BK-20260304-001\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.cancel(31L, \"BK-20260304-001\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER))\n            .isInstanceOf(BookingNotFoundException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_revert_booking_to_confirmed_when_stripe_refund_fails() throws Exception {\n        Instant slotStart = Instant.now().plusSeconds(72 * 3600);\n        try (SlotTimingServer server = startSlotTimingServer(21L, slotStart)) {\n            CancellationService service = createService(server.baseUrl(), 24, 0);\n            Booking booking = confirmedBooking(91L, \"BK-20260304-001\", 31L, 11L, 21L, 150000L);\n            when(bookingRepository.findByBookingRef(\"BK-20260304-001\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n            when(cancellationRequestRepository.save(any(CancellationRequest.class)))\n                .thenAnswer(invocation -> invocation.getArgument(0));\n            when(stripeRefundService.createRefund(any(StripeRefundRequest.class)))\n                .thenThrow(new StripeIntegrationException(\"stripe down\"));\n\n            assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.cancel(31L, \"BK-20260304-001\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER))\n                .isInstanceOf(RefundFailedException.class);\n\n            assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n            ArgumentCaptor<CancellationRequest> requestCaptor = ArgumentCaptor.forClass(CancellationRequest.class);\n            verify(cancellationRequestRepository).save(requestCaptor.capture());\n            assertThat(requestCaptor.getValue().getStatus()).isEqualTo(CancellationRequestStatus.REJECTED);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_finalise_after_refund_by_voiding_tickets_and_publishing_booking_cancelled_event() {\n        CancellationService service = createService(\"http://localhost:9\", 24, 0);\n        Booking booking = confirmedBooking(91L, \"BK-20260304-001\", 31L, 11L, 21L, 150000L);\n        booking.markCancellationPending();\n\n        BookingItem bookingItem = new BookingItem(91L, 101L, null, \"TC-1\", 150000L, \"inr\");\n        ETicket ticket = new ETicket(91L, 501L, \"qr\", \"/tickets/BK-20260304-001/501.pdf\");\n        Refund refund = new Refund(91L, \"re_123\", 150000L, \"inr\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER, RefundStatus.PENDING);\n        CancellationRequest request = new CancellationRequest(91L, 31L, RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER);\n\n        when(bookingRepository.findById(91L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n        when(bookingItemRepository.findByBookingId(91L)).thenReturn(List.of(bookingItem));\n        when(eTicketRepository.findByBookingId(91L)).thenReturn(List.of(ticket));\n        when(refundRepository.findByBookingId(91L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(refund));\n        when(cancellationRequestRepository.findTopByBookingIdOrderByRequestedAtDesc(91L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(request));\n\n        service.finaliseAfterRefund(91L);\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n        assertThat(bookingItem.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingItemStatus.CANCELLED);\n        assertThat(ticket.getStatus()).isEqualTo(ETicketStatus.VOIDED);\n        assertThat(refund.getStatus()).isEqualTo(RefundStatus.SUCCEEDED);\n        assertThat(request.getStatus()).isEqualTo(CancellationRequestStatus.APPROVED);\n        verify(afterCommitEventPublisher).publish(any(BookingCancelledEvent.class));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_do_nothing_when_finalise_after_refund_called_for_already_cancelled_booking() {\n        CancellationService service = createService(\"http://localhost:9\", 24, 0);\n        Booking booking = confirmedBooking(91L, \"BK-20260304-001\", 31L, 11L, 21L, 150000L);\n        booking.markCancellationPending();\n        booking.cancel();\n\n        when(bookingRepository.findById(91L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n        service.finaliseAfterRefund(91L);\n\n        verify(bookingItemRepository, never()).findByBookingId(any());\n        verify(eTicketRepository, never()).findByBookingId(any());\n        verify(afterCommitEventPublisher, never()).publish(any());\n    }\n\n    private CancellationService createService(String baseUrl, int windowHours, int feePercent) {\n        return new CancellationService(\n            bookingRepository,\n            bookingItemRepository,\n            eTicketRepository,\n            refundRepository,\n            cancellationRequestRepository,\n            stripeRefundService,\n            afterCommitEventPublisher,\n            baseUrl,\n            windowHours,\n            feePercent\n        );\n    }\n\n    private Booking confirmedBooking(Long id, String ref, Long userId, Long cartId, Long slotId, Long totalAmount) {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(ref, cartId, userId, 44L, slotId, totalAmount, \"inr\");\n        ReflectionTestUtils.setField(booking, \"id\", id);\n        booking.confirm(\"pi_123\", \"ch_123\");\n        return booking;\n    }\n\n    private SlotTimingServer startSlotTimingServer(Long slotId, Instant startTime) throws IOException {\n        HttpServer server = HttpServer.create(new InetSocketAddress(0), 0);\n        AtomicReference<String> bodyRef = new AtomicReference<>();\n        server.createContext(\"/internal/scheduling/slots/\" + slotId + \"/timing\", exchange -> {\n            String body = \"{\\\"slotId\\\":\" + slotId + \",\\\"startTime\\\":\\\"\" + startTime + \"\\\"}\";\n            bodyRef.set(body);\n            byte[] payload = body.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);\n            exchange.getResponseHeaders().add(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\");\n            exchange.sendResponseHeaders(200, payload.length);\n            try (OutputStream os = exchange.getResponseBody()) {\n                os.write(payload);\n            }\n        });\n        server.start();\n        return new SlotTimingServer(server, \"http://localhost:\" + server.getAddress().getPort());\n    }\n\n    private record SlotTimingServer(HttpServer server, String baseUrl) implements AutoCloseable {\n        @Override\n        public void close() {\n            server.stop(0);\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

assertThat(requestCaptor.getValue().amount()).isEqualTo(135000L);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_keep_booking_cancellation_pending_when_refund_is_async_pending() throws Exception {\n assertThat(response.status()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING);\n            assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING);\n            verify(afterCommitEventPublisher, never()).publish(any());\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_cancellation_window_has_closed() throws Exception {\n        Instant slotStart = assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.cancel(31L, \"BK-20260304-001\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER))\n            .isInstanceOf(CancellationNotAllowedException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_cancelling_booking_already_cancelled() {\n        CancellationService service = createService(\"http://localhost:9\", 24, 0);\n        Booking booking = confirmedBooking(91L, \"BK-20260304-001\", 31L, 11L, 21L, 150000L);\n        booking.markCancellationPending();\n        booking.cancel();\n        when(bookingRepository.findByBookingRef(\"BK-20260304-001\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.cancel(31L, \"BK-20260304-001\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER))\n            .isInstanceOf(CancellationNotAllowedException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_booking_not_found_when_user_is_not_owner() {\n        CancellationService service = createService(\"http://localhost:9\", 24, 0);\n        Booking booking = confirmedBooking(91L, \"BK-20260304-001\", 77L, 11L, 21L, 150000L);\n        when(bookingRepository.findByBookingRef(\"BK-20260304-001\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.cancel(31L, \"BK-20260304-001\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER))\n            .isInstanceOf(BookingNotFoundException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_revert_booking_to_confirmed_when_stripe_refund_fails() throws Exception {\n        Instant slotStart = Instant.now().plusSeconds(72 * 3600);\n        try (SlotTimingServer server = startSlotTimingServer(21L, slotStart)) {\n            CancellationService service = createService(server.baseUrl(), 24, 0);\n            Booking booking = confirmedBooking(91L, \"BK-20260304-001\", 31L, 11L, 21L, 150000L);\n            when(bookingRepository.findByBookingRef(\"BK-20260304-001\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n            when(cancellationRequestRepository.save(any(CancellationRequest.class)))\n                .thenAnswer(invocation -> invocation.getArgument(0));\n            when(stripeRefundService.createRefund(any(StripeRefundRequest.class)))\n                .thenThrow(new StripeIntegrationException(\"stripe down\"));\n\n            assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.cancel(31L, \"BK-20260304-001\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER))\n                .isInstanceOf(RefundFailedException.class);\n\n            assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n            ArgumentCaptor<CancellationRequest> requestCaptor = ArgumentCaptor.forClass(CancellationRequest.class);\n            verify(cancellationRequestRepository).save(requestCaptor.capture());\n            assertThat(requestCaptor.getValue().getStatus()).isEqualTo(CancellationRequestStatus.REJECTED);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_finalise_after_refund_by_voiding_tickets_and_publishing_booking_cancelled_event() {\n        CancellationService service = createService(\"http://localhost:9\", 24, 0);\n        Booking booking = confirmedBooking(91L, \"BK-20260304-001\", 31L, 11L, 21L, 150000L);\n        booking.markCancellationPending();\n\n        BookingItem bookingItem = new BookingItem(91L, 101L, null, \"TC-1\", 150000L, \"inr\");\n        ETicket ticket = new ETicket(91L, 501L, \"qr\", \"/tickets/BK-20260304-001/501.pdf\");\n        Refund refund = new Refund(91L, \"re_123\", 150000L, \"inr\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER, RefundStatus.PENDING);\n        CancellationRequest request = new CancellationRequest(91L, 31L, RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER);\n\n        when(bookingRepository.findById(91L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n        when(bookingItemRepository.findByBookingId(91L)).thenReturn(List.of(bookingItem));\n        when(eTicketRepository.findByBookingId(91L)).thenReturn(List.of(ticket));\n        when(refundRepository.findByBookingId(91L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(refund));\n        when(cancellationRequestRepository.findTopByBookingIdOrderByRequestedAtDesc(91L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(request));\n\n        service.finaliseAfterRefund(91L);\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n        assertThat(bookingItem.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingItemStatus.CANCELLED);\n        assertThat(ticket.getStatus()).isEqualTo(ETicketStatus.VOIDED);\n        assertThat(refund.getStatus()).isEqualTo(RefundStatus.SUCCEEDED);\n        assertThat(request.getStatus()).isEqualTo(CancellationRequestStatus.APPROVED);\n        verify(afterCommitEventPublisher).publish(any(BookingCancelledEvent.class));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_do_nothing_when_finalise_after_refund_called_for_already_cancelled_booking() {\n        CancellationService service = createService(\"http://localhost:9\", 24, 0);\n        Booking booking = confirmedBooking(91L, \"BK-20260304-001\", 31L, 11L, 21L, 150000L);\n        booking.markCancellationPending();\n        booking.cancel();\n\n        when(bookingRepository.findById(91L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n        service.finaliseAfterRefund(91L);\n\n        verify(bookingItemRepository, never()).findByBookingId(any());\n        verify(eTicketRepository, never()).findByBookingId(any());\n        verify(afterCommitEventPublisher, never()).publish(any());\n    }\n\n    private CancellationService createService(String baseUrl, int windowHours, int feePercent) {\n        return new CancellationService(\n            bookingRepository,\n            bookingItemRepository,\n            eTicketRepository,\n            refundRepository,\n            cancellationRequestRepository,\n            stripeRefundService,\n            afterCommitEventPublisher,\n            baseUrl,\n            windowHours,\n            feePercent\n        );\n    }\n\n    private Booking confirmedBooking(Long id, String ref, Long userId, Long cartId, Long slotId, Long totalAmount) {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(ref, cartId, userId, 44L, slotId, totalAmount, \"inr\");\n        ReflectionTestUtils.setField(booking, \"id\", id);\n        booking.confirm(\"pi_123\", \"ch_123\");\n        return booking;\n    }\n\n    private SlotTimingServer startSlotTimingServer(Long slotId, Instant startTime) throws IOException {\n        HttpServer server = HttpServer.create(new InetSocketAddress(0), 0);\n        AtomicReference<String> bodyRef = new AtomicReference<>();\n        server.createContext(\"/internal/scheduling/slots/\" + slotId + \"/timing\", exchange -> {\n            String body = \"{\\\"slotId\\\":\" + slotId + \",\\\"startTime\\\":\\\"\" + startTime + \"\\\"}\";\n            bodyRef.set(body);\n            byte[] payload = body.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);\n            exchange.getResponseHeaders().add(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\");\n            exchange.sendResponseHeaders(200, payload.length);\n            try (OutputStream os = exchange.getResponseBody()) {\n                os.write(payload);\n            }\n        });\n        server.start();\n        return new SlotTimingServer(server, \"http://localhost:\" + server.getAddress().getPort());\n    }\n\n    private record SlotTimingServer(HttpServer server, String baseUrl) implements AutoCloseable {\n        @Override\n        public void close() {\n            server.stop(0);\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

  .thenThrow(new CancellationNotAllowedException(\"Cancellation window has closed\", Map.of(\"windowClosedAt\", \"2026-03-04T08:00:00Z\")));\n\n        mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/v1/bookings/BK-20260304-001/cancel\")\n                .with(authentication(userAuthentication()))\n                .contentType(\"application/json\")\n                .content(\"{\\\"reason\\\":\\\"REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER\\\"}\"))\n            .andExpect(status().isConflict())\n            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.errorCode\").value(\"CANCELLATION_NOT_ALLOWED\"));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_return_409_when_booking_status_not_confirmed_for_cancellation() throws Exception {\n        when(cancellationService.cancel(1L, \"BK-20260304-001\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER))\n            .thenThrow(new CancellationNotAllowedException(\"Booking is not in CONFIRMED state\", Map.of(\"currentStatus\", \"PENDING\")));\n\n        mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/v1/bookings/BK-20260304-001/cancel\")\n                .with(authentication(userAuthentication()))\n                .contentType(\"application/json\")\n                .content(\"{\\\"reason\\\":\\\"REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER\\\"}\"))\n            .andExpect(status().isConflict());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_return_404_when_non_owner_attempts_cancellation() throws Exception {\n        when(cancellationService.cancel(1L, \"BK-20260304-001\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER))\n            .thenThrow(new BookingNotFoundException(\"BK-20260304-001\"));\n\n        mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/v1/bookings/BK-20260304-001/cancel\")\n                .with(authentication(userAuthentication()))\n                .contentType(\"application/json\")\n                .content(\"{\\\"reason\\\":\\\"REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER\\\"}\"))\n            .andExpect(status().isNotFound())\n            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.errorCode\").value(\"BOOKING_NOT_FOUND\"));\n    }\n\n    private UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken userAuthentication() {\n        return new UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken(\n            new AuthenticatedUser(1L, \"USER\", null, null),\n            null,\n            List.of(new SimpleGrantedAuthority(\"ROLE_USER\"))\n        );\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

    "output": "Chunk ID: 3edf35\nWall time: 0.0511 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 652\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain;\n\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThatThrownBy;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.BookingStatus;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.BusinessRuleException;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n\nclass BookingTest {\n\n    @Test\n    void should_transition_pending_to_confirmed_when_confirm_called() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(\"BK-20260304-001\", 1L, 2L, 3L, 4L, 5000L, \"inr\");\n\n        booking.confirm(\"pi_123\", \"ch_123\");\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n        assertThat(booking.getStripePaymentIntentId()).isEqualTo(\"pi_123\");\n        assertThat(booking.getStripeChargeId()).isEqualTo(\"ch_123\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_transition_confirmed_to_cancellation_pending_when_mark_cancellation_pending_called() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(\"BK-20260304-001\", 1L, 2L, 3L, 4L, 5000L, \"inr\");\n        booking.confirm(\"pi_123\", \"ch_123\");\n\n        booking.markCancellationPending();\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_transition_cancellation_pending_to_cancelled_when_cancel_called() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(\"BK-20260304-001\", 1L, 2L, 3L, 4L, 5000L, \"inr\");\n        booking.confirm(\"pi_123\", \"ch_123\");\n        booking.markCancellationPending();\n\n        booking.cancel();\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_transitioning_confirmed_to_cancelled_directly() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(\"BK-20260304-001\", 1L, 2L, 3L, 4L, 5000L, \"inr\");\n        booking.confirm(\"pi_123\", \"ch_123\");\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::cancel)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"cannot be cancelled\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_transitioning_cancelled_to_any_state() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(\"BK-20260304-001\", 1L, 2L, 3L, 4L, 5000L, \"inr\");\n        booking.confirm(\"pi_123\", \"ch_123\");\n        booking.markCancellationPending();\n        booking.cancel();\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> booking.confirm(\"pi_999\", \"ch_999\"))\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class);\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::markCancellationPending)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class);\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::cancel)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class);\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

when(orgOAuthService.buildAuthorizationUrl(1L)).thenReturn(\"http://auth?state=abc\");\n\n        mockMvc.perform(post(\"/api/v1/admin/orgs/1/eventbrite/connect\"))\n            .andExpect(status().isOk())\n            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.authorizationUrl\").value(\"http://auth?state=abc\"));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @WithMockUser(roles = \"USER\")\n    void @Test\n    void should_return_400_when_oauth_state_invalid() throws Exception {\n        when(orgOAuthService.handleCallback(eq(1L), eq(\"code\"), eq(\"bad\")))\n            .thenThrow(new mockMvc.perform(get(\"/api/v1/admin/orgs/1/eventbrite/callback\")\n                .queryParam(\"code\", \"code\")\n                .queryParam(\"state\", \"state\"))\n            .andExpect(status().isBadGateway());\n    }\n}\n"

## Entry 479

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T20:23:45.138Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_JT79OZqZA4aiJtgP7ACfkx7q",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 6782a1\nWall time: 0.0511 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1103\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain;\n\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThatThrownBy;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.RefundReason;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.RefundStatus;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.BusinessRuleException;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n\nclass RefundTest {\n\n    @Test\n    void should_transition_pending_to_succeeded_when_update_status_called() {\n        Refund refund = new Refund(1L, \"re_123\", 1000L, \"inr\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER, RefundStatus.PENDING);\n\n        refund.updateStatus(RefundStatus.SUCCEEDED);\n\n        assertThat(refund.getStatus()).isEqualTo(RefundStatus.SUCCEEDED);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_transition_pending_to_failed_when_update_status_called() {\n        Refund refund = new Refund(1L, \"re_123\", 1000L, \"inr\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER, RefundStatus.PENDING);\n\n        refund.updateStatus(RefundStatus.FAILED);\n\n        assertThat(refund.getStatus()).isEqualTo(RefundStatus.FAILED);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_transitioning_succeeded_to_failed() {\n        Refund refund = new Refund(1L, \"re_123\", 1000L, \"inr\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER, RefundStatus.SUCCEEDED);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> refund.updateStatus(RefundStatus.FAILED))\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"terminal\");\n    }\n}\npackage com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.service;\n\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.never;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.verify;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.when;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.Refund;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.RefundReason;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.RefundStatus;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.repository.RefundRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.dto.StripeRefundResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.service.StripeRefundService;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ExtendWith;\nimport org.mockito.Mock;\nimport org.mockito.junit.jupiter.MockitoExtension;\n\n@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)\nclass RefundServiceTest {\n\n    @Mock\n    private RefundRepository refundRepository;\n    @Mock\n    private CancellationService cancellationService;\n    @Mock\n    private StripeRefundService stripeRefundService;\n\n    private RefundService refundService;\n\n    @BeforeEach\n    void setUp() {\n        refundService = new RefundService(refundRepository, cancellationService, stripeRefundService);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_call_finalise_after_refund_when_webhook_status_is_succeeded() {\n        Refund refund = new Refund(91L, \"re_123\", 1000L, \"inr\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER, RefundStatus.PENDING);\n        when(refundRepository.findByStripeRefundId(\"re_123\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(refund));\n        when(stripeRefundService.getRefund(\"re_123\")).thenReturn(new StripeRefundResponse(\"re_123\", \"succeeded\", 1000L, \"inr\"));\n\n        refundService.updateRefundStatus(\"re_123\");\n\n        verify(cancellationService).finaliseAfterRefund(91L);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_handle_failed_refund_without_publishing_cancel_event() {\n        Refund refund = new Refund(91L, \"re_123\", 1000L, \"inr\", RefundReason.REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER, RefundStatus.PENDING);\n        when(refundRepository.findByStripeRefundId(\"re_123\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(refund));\n        when(stripeRefundService.getRefund(\"re_123\")).thenReturn(new StripeRefundResponse(\"re_123\", \"failed\", 1000L, \"inr\"));\n\n        refundService.updateRefundStatus(\"re_123\");\n\n        verify(cancellationService).handleRefundFailed(\"re_123\");\n {

    "call_id": "call_E6sMK1KKcpYPYiMVTVqKedEQ",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 84858b\nWall time: 0.0507 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 4649\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.service;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.dto.response.CancelItemsResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.dto.response.CancellationQuoteResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.dto.response.CancellationResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.dto.response.RefundResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.Booking;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.BookingItem;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.CancellationRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.ETicket;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.Refund;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.BookingItemStatus;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.BookingStatus;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.CancellationRequestStatus;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.RefundCancellationType;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.RefundReason;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.RefundStatus;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.exception.BookingNotFoundException;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.exception.CancellationNotAllowedException;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.exception.DuplicateItemCancellationException;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.exception.InvalidCancelItemsRequestException;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.exception.RefundFailedException;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.repository.BookingItemRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.repository.BookingRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.repository.CancellationRequestRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.repository.ETicketRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.repository.RefundRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.BookingCancelledEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.dto.StripeRefundRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.dto.StripeRefundResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.exception.StripeIntegrationException;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.service.StripeRefundService;\nimport java.util.HashMap;\nimport java.util.LinkedHashSet;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.Objects;\nimport java.util.Set;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\nimport org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;\n\n@Service\npublic class CancellationService {\n\n    private final BookingRepository bookingRepository;\n    private final BookingItemRepository bookingItemRepository;\n    private final ETicketRepository eTicketRepository;\n    private final RefundRepository refundRepository;\n    private final CancellationRequestRepository cancellationRequestRepository;\n    private final StripeRefundService stripeRefundService;\n    private final AfterCommitEventPublisher afterCommitEventPublisher;\n    private final CancellationPolicyService cancellationPolicyService;\n\n    public CancellationService(\n        BookingRepository bookingRepository,\n        BookingItemRepository bookingItemRepository,\n        ETicketRepository eTicketRepository,\n        RefundRepository refundRepository,\n        CancellationRequestRepository cancellationRequestRepository,\n        StripeRefundService stripeRefundService,\n        AfterCommitEventPublisher afterCommitEventPublisher,\n        CancellationPolicyService cancellationPolicyService\n    ) {\n        this.bookingRepository = bookingRepository;\n        this.bookingItemRepository = bookingItemRepository;\n        this.eTicketRepository = eTicketRepository;\n        this.refundRepository = refundRepository;\n        this.cancellationRequestRepository = cancellationRequestRepository;\n        this.stripeRefundService = stripeRefundService;\n        this.afterCommitEventPublisher = afterCommitEventPublisher;\n        this.cancellationPolicyService = cancellationPolicyService;\n    }\n\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\n    public CancellationQuoteResponse computeQuote(Long userId, String bookingRef, List<Long> requestedBookingItemIds) {\n        Booking booking = getOwnedBooking(userId, bookingRef);\n        ensureBookingConfirmed(booking);\n\n        List<BookingItem> selectedItems = resolveSelectedActiveItems(booking.getId(), requestedBookingItemIds);\n        long requestedAmount = selectedItems.stream().mapToLong(BookingItem::getUnitPrice).sum();\n\n        RefundCalculationResult quote = cancellationPolicyService.calculateRefund(booking, requestedAmount);\n        return new CancellationQuoteResponse(\n            booking.getBookingRef(),\n            quote.tierLabel(),\n            quote.refundPercent(),\n            requestedAmount,\n            quote.refundAmountInSmallestUnit(),\n            booking.getCurrency()\n        );\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public CancelItemsResponse cancelItems(Long userId, String bookingRef, List<Long> requestedBookingItemIds, RefundReason reason) {\n        Booking booking = getOwnedBooking(userId, bookingRef);\n        ensureBookingConfirmed(booking);\n\n        List<BookingItem> allItems = bookingItemRepository.findByBookingId(booking.getId());\n        List<BookingItem> activeItems = allItems.stream()\n            .filter(item -> item.getStatus() == BookingItemStatus.ACTIVE)\n            .toList();\n\n        if (activeItems.isEmpty()) {\n            throw new CancellationNotAllowedException(\"Booking has no active items to cancel\");\n        }\n\n        List<BookingItem> selectedItems = resolveSelectedItemsFromActive(activeItems, requestedBookingItemIds);\n\n        for (BookingItem item : selectedItems) {\n            if (cancellationRequestRepository.existsByBookingItemIdAndStatus(item.getId(), CancellationRequestStatus.PENDING)) {\n                throw new DuplicateItemCancellationException(item.getId());\n            }\n        }\n\n        long requestedAmount = selectedItems.stream().mapToLong(BookingItem::getUnitPrice).sum();\n        RefundCalculationResult calculation = cancellationPolicyService.calculateRefund(booking, requestedAmount);\n        long refundAmount = calculation.refundAmountInSmallestUnit();\n\n        selectedItems.forEach(item -> cancellationRequestRepository.save(new CancellationRequest(\n            booking.getId(),\n            item.getId(),\n            userId,\n            reason\n        )));\n\n        if (selectedItems.size() == activeItems.size()) {\n            booking.markCancellationPending();\n        }\n\n        Refund refund;\n        try {\n            refund = createRefundRecord(\n                booking,\n                reason,\n                refundAmount,\n                RefundCancellationType.BUYER_PARTIAL\n            );\n        } catch (StripeIntegrationException ex) {\n            booking.revertCancellationPending();\n            throw new RefundFailedException(\"Unable to process refund at this time. Please contact support.\", ex.getMessage());\n        }\n\n        List<Long> cancelledSeatIds = cancelItemsAndVoidTickets(selectedItems);\n        cancellationRequestRepository.findByBookingIdAndStatus(booking.getId(), CancellationRequestStatus.PENDING).stream()\n            .filter(request -> request.getBookingItemId() != null)\n            .filter(request -> selectedItems.stream().anyMatch(item -> item.getId().equals(request.getBookingItemId())))\n            .forEach(CancellationRequest::approve);\n\n        long remainingTotal = allItems.stream()\n            .filter(item -> selectedItems.stream().noneMatch(cancelled -> cancelled.getId().equals(item.getId())))\n            .filter(item -> item.getStatus() == BookingItemStatus.ACTIVE)\n            .mapToLong(BookingItem::getUnitPrice)\n            .sum();\n        booking.updateTotalAmount(remainingTotal);\n\n        long activeRemaining = bookingItemRepository.findByBookingId(booking.getId()).stream()\n            .filter(item -> item.getStatus() == BookingItemStatus.ACTIVE)\n            .count();\n        if (activeRemaining == 0) {\n            booking.cancelFromConfirmed();\n        }\n\n        publishBookingCancelled(booking, cancelledSeatIds, \"BUYER_CANCEL\");\n\n        return new CancelItemsResponse(\n            booking.getBookingRef(),\n            booking.getStatus(),\n            selectedItems.stream().map(BookingItem::getId).toList(),\n            new CancelItemsResponse.ItemCancellationRefundResponse(\n                refund.getCancellationType().name(),\n                calculation.refundPercent(),\n                refund.getAmount(),\n                refund.getCurrency(),\n                refund.getStatus().name()\n            )\n        );\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public CancellationResponse cancel(Long userId, String bookingRef, RefundReason reason) {\n        Booking booking = getOwnedBooking(userId, bookingRef);\n        ensureBookingConfirmed(booking);\n\n        List<BookingItem> activeItems = bookingItemRepository.findByBookingId(booking.getId()).stream()\n            .filter(item -> item.getStatus() == BookingItemStatus.ACTIVE)\n            .toList();\n        if (activeItems.isEmpty()) {\n            throw new CancellationNotAllowedException(\"Booking has (StripeIntegrationException ex) {\n            booking.revertCancellationPending();\n booking.updateTotalAmount(0L);\n\n        cancellationRequestRepository.findTopByBookingIdOrderByRequestedAtDesc(booking.getId()).ifPresent(CancellationRequest::approve);\n        publishBookingCancelled(booking, seatIds, \"BUYER_CANCEL\");\n\n        return new CancellationResponse(\n            booking.getBookingRef(),\n            booking.getStatus(),\n            new
